items.claim: Setup block: CMRS identification (Caron-Huot, Mazac, Rastelli, Simmons-Duffin; arXiv:2102.08951 v2 May 2021; JHEP 07 (2021) 110), amplitude eq (1)/(2.1) with 8piG (no kappa^2), -lambda_3^2, -lambda_4, g_k on A^aB^b basis (J=2a+2b, 2k=4a+6b, g_6/g_6' degeneracy), partial-wave conventions P_J=2F1(-J,J+D-3,(D-2)/2,(1-x)/2) and n_J^(D)=2^D pi^((D-2)/2)/Gamma((D-2)/2)*(J+1)_(D-4)(2J+D-3), 0<=rho_J<=2 with only positivity used, heavy average eq (18), assumptions (i) upper-half-plane analyticity and (ii) nonperturbative spin-2 Regge boundedness stronger than CRG, sum rules C_{k,u} eqs (22)-(23) with subtraction points s'=0,-u, selection rule eq (24) (J>=k, b<=k/2; lambda_3, lambda_4 drop from all C_{k>=2}; graviton only in C_2 via -8piG/u), C_2 eq (20), improved sum rule eqs (42)-(44), nulls eq (51), LP eqs (46)-(47),(52) with Bessel scaling limit eqs (48)-(49), impact-parameter transform eqs (37)-(38), C_4^improved eq (57), SUSY sector eqs (72),(73),(77), dimensions of results (headline D=5..12, detailed studies D=6, sugra D=10, D=4 leading-log only)
items.detail: Verified line-by-line against the arXiv v2 LaTeX source (downloaded e-print, files sections/sec_dispersion_review.tex, sec_localized_b.tex, sec_introduction.tex; local copies under /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-mruckman1-Desktop-dev-quantum-gravity/31217463-cf79-4bf7-a0e6-c2b5dbd908b3/scratchpad/cmrs/). Every formula matches verbatim, including the exact heavy block of C_2^improved (eq 3.8): (2m^2+u)P_J(1+2u/m^2)/(m^2(m^2+u)^2) - (u^2/m^6)[(4m^2+3u)P_J(1)/(m^2+u)^2 + 4u P_J'(1)/(m^4-u^2)]. I additionally RE-DERIVED this block independently: symbolically resumming sum_{n>=2}(n u^{2n-2} C_{2n,0} + u^{2n-1} C'_{2n,0}) heavy blocks in sympy reproduces the subtraction term exactly (difference = 0 for u^2<m^4). The claims use global equation numbering while the compiled paper uses per-section numbering; the mapping (2.x -> 1-36, 3.x -> 37-79, A.x -> 80+) is internally consistent for every cited number (checked via main.aux labels: 2.1, 2.14, 2.18, 2.20-2.25, 3.1-3.2, 3.6-3.8, 3.15-3.16, 3.37-3.42, A.1-A.11). Intro confirms Regge assumption is postulated to hold nonperturbatively and is 'a little stronger than' the Classical Regge Growth conjecture's O(s^2). arXiv abs page independently confirms authors, v1 = Feb 17 2021, v2 = May 24 2021, DOI 10.1007/JHEP07(2021)110. Caveat: 'C_2^imp valid for u in (-M^2, 0]' — paper says mostly restrict to u in (-M^2,0]; validity at u=-M^2 and beyond only for meromorphic amplitudes (Appendix B), as the claims' parameters block correctly states.

Formula matches paper eq 2.35 verbatim (source sec_dispersion_review.tex line 294). Recomputed numerics: kappa(4)=10.612487, kappa(5)=9.0938314, kappa(6)=8.3384620, kappa(10)=7.2178886 — all four quoted values correct to the digits given. INDEPENDENT re-derivation: I solved the convex-hull LP from scratch (J=2 at m=M vs J=4 at m_4, Casimir J^2=J(J+D-3), null component J^2(2J^2-5D+4)/m^4, x-axis intersection minimized over m_4 on a fine grid) and reproduced -kappa(D) to ~3e-7 in D=4,5,6,10, confirming the closed form is correct, not just correctly copied. NOTE: 'J^2' in the null constraint must be read as the SO(D-1) Casimir J(J+D-3) (paper's script-J-squared), not J squared — the hull check only works with the Casimir. Consistency note: kappa(4)=10.612 gives lower bound -10.612, indeed slightly weaker than the quoted CHVD optimal -10.3466 (the -10.3466 provenance itself is the caller's prior validation, not re-verified here; only the abstract of 2011.02957 was accessible, but the direction — CMRS single-null bound must be weaker than or equal to CHVD's multi-null optimum — is arithmetically consistent). g_2, g_3<=3/M^2 (eq 2.31), g_4 bound (eq 2.36) all verbatim in source.

items.claim: Target 2 (non-gravitational g_3-g_4 region via smeared wavepackets == forward-limit region, D=6, Fig 3): 17-dim Table 2 col 1 space + C_{4,u=0} vs 33-dim CHVD forward-limit space; regions 'essentially identical'
items.detail: Figure caption in source (sec_localized_b.tex, fig g3_g4_without_gravity_d_6) matches the claim exactly: 'the blue region uses derivatives around the forward limit as in [CHVD] (with a 33-dimensional space of functionals), while the yellow region uses small impact parameter wavepackets (built from the 17-dimensional space of functionals listed in Table 2, together with C_{4,u=0}). The two regions are essentially identical and appear overlapping in the plot.' The claim correctly notes no number is given. This is a faithful extraction; the region itself was not independently recomputed here (would require the full smeared LP), but the reference region is CHVD machinery the caller has already validated.

items.claim: Target 3 (HEADLINE Table 3, D=5..12): first inequality exact slope-3 edge g_2 - g_3/3 + c_0(D) >= 0 with c_0 = 60.3086/28.0546/18.0717/13.7186/11.1250/9.5208/8.4687/7.9016; constraint counts 17/11/7/13/5/3/7/3; implied polygon tips min g_2 M^2/(8piG) = -17.886/-9.731/-6.913/-5.605/-4.794/-4.251/-3.887/-3.691; example D=6 inequality g_2 + 0.0940931 g_3 + 4.55838 >= 0; settings 17 functionals, J_max=42, dx=1/400, converged to 1e-4
items.detail: Table 3 (app_flat_space_numerics.tex, label eq:inequalitiesforfigure1) matches VERBATIM: all eight c_0(D) values, all coefficient pairs, the D=6 example inequality exactly as quoted, and the constraint counts 17/11/7/13/5/3/7/3 (recounted from source). I independently recomputed the polygon tips by minimizing g_2 over the intersection of each dimension's inequalities: -17.8856, -9.7308, -6.9131, -5.6046, -4.7940, -4.2507, -3.8871, -3.6910 — every claimed tip correct to the 1e-3 precision quoted. Slope-3 upper edge explained in text via C_2^imp[m^2,J=0] = 2/m^4 + 3p^2/m^6, matching. Settings confirmed in Table 2 col 1: functionals {p^n C_2^imp | n<=7.5} + {p^i X_4 | i=0..5} + {p^i X_6 | i=0..3}, J_max=42 (paper: raising J_max changes bounds by less than fraction 1e-4), dx=1/400, precision=768. Minor caveat inherited from the paper: '17-dimensional' is exact for even D (7 half-integer powers 1.5..7.5 + 6 + 4); for D=5 (basis p^3-p^2,...) and odd D>=7 (basis p^2,p^3,...) the n<=7.5 rule gives 5-6 C_2^imp functionals, and the paper only says the precise D-dependent combinations follow Table 1 — so 'seventeen' should not be taken literally in odd D when reproducing.

items.claim: Target 4 (refined min g_2, D=6): g_2 M^2/(8piG) = -9.57 with 92-dim space (93 projected to kill g_3); Fig 7 caption tip approx -9.6; 17-functional Table-3 value -9.73; extremal spectrum single state at J=2,4,6, nearly-flat leading trajectory, lower triangle (opposite to string theory); bounds converged to 1e-4 in cutoffs and within a few percent of LP optimum
items.detail: Fig 6 caption (source): 'The extremal value of g_2 corresponding to this spectrum is g_2 M^2/(8piG) = -9.57', computed with the 92-dimensional space; Table 2 caption confirms projection from 93 to 92 dimensions onto the g_3-blind subspace. Fig 7 caption: tip of the cone at 'g_2 M^2/(8piG) ~ -9.6'. My LP recomputation of the Table 3 polygon gives the 17-functional tip -9.7308, matching the claimed -9.73 (and correctly weaker than -9.57, as expected when enlarging the functional space). Spectrum description matches source text: 'there is only a single state at spins J=2,4,6', 'minimal value of m^2 appears to be nearly flat as a function of J', and Fig 6 caption: 'In string theory, the spectrum would occupy the upper triangle, but here it is in the lower triangle.' Convergence statements ('less than the fraction 1e-4' in J_max for Fig 4; 'within a few percent of optimal' for LP (52)) verbatim in source. The gloss m^2 <~ M^2 J/2 is the extractor's reading of the plot (the exact trajectory M^2(J-2)/2 appears only in the extended-u Appendix B spectra); treat that particular expression as approximate, not a paper-quoted formula.

items.claim: Target 5 (higher contacts, D=6): C_4^improved isolates 4g_4 - 2u g_5 + u^2 g_6'; regions computed at g_2=0, g_2M^2/(8piG)=-5,-9; as g_2 -> min (~-9.6) region shrinks to point (g_4M^6, g_5M^8, g_6'M^10)/(8piG) approx (1.4, -34, 165); functionals = Table 2 col 1 + {p^i C_4^imp, i=0..5}, g_3-independent subspace
items.detail: All verbatim in source: the C_4^improved low-energy side '4g_4 - 2u g_5 + g_6' u^2' (eqs 3.20-3.21 = global 56-57, including the full heavy block with P_J, P_J', P_J'' terms); Fig 7 caption: g_2 values 0 (lightest blue), -5, -9 (darkest blue); 'the allowed region shrinks towards (g_4M^6/8piG, g_5M^8/8piG, g_6'M^10/8piG) ~ (1.4, -34, 165)' when g_2 approaches ~-9.6; 'same parameters and functionals listed in column 1 of Table 2, with the additional functionals C_4^imp x {p^0,...,p^5}'; 'independent of g_3 by restricting to the subspace of functionals whose EFT contribution is independent of g_3'. Also confirmed: g_6 (the A^3 contact) is deliberately excluded because it is measured by C_6. As the claim itself flags, (1.4,-34,165) is a 2-significant-figure caption readout — appropriate only as a loose target. Not independently recomputed (requires full two-improved-sum-rule LP).

items.claim: Target 6 (maximal sugra D=10): M_susy = 8piG/(stu) + g_0 + g_2(...) with s^2 M -> 0 Regge; C_{-2}: 8piG/(-u) = <m^2(2m^2+u)P_J>; C_0^improved: g_0 = <(2m^2+u)P_J/(m^2+u) - 2u^2/(m^4-u^2)>; bound 0 <= g_0 <= 3.000 * 8piG/M^6 via 14 functionals (C_{-2} x p^2..p^10, C_0^imp x 4 powers, X_2 x 2 powers); type II check g_0 M^6/(8piG) = 2 zeta(3) ~ 2.4041 <= 3.000
items.detail: All formulas and the 3.000 bound verbatim in source (eqs 3.35-3.43 = global 71-79; '0 <= g_0 <= 3.000 8piG/M^6 (D=10, maximal supergravity)'). INDEPENDENT checks: (1) I recomputed g_0 for type II symbolically: stu*M_susy = exp(2 sum zeta(2k+1)(s^{2k+1}+t^{2k+1}+u^{2k+1})/(2k+1)) = 1 + 2 zeta(3) stu + ..., so g_0 = 2 zeta(3) = 2.40411... exactly — confirmed. (2) Independent-method corroboration: arXiv 2506.09884 (crossing-symmetric dispersion relations, different machinery) reports g_0 M^6/(8piG) <= 2.97 for the same problem with smearing up to p_max = M — consistent with (slightly stronger than) 3.000, confirming the number is the right scale and not a transcription artifact. ONE ARITHMETIC FLAG inherited verbatim from the paper: p^2,...,p^10 is nine powers, so 9+4+2 = 15, yet the paper (and the claim) says 'a total of 14 functionals'. The paper's own count and its enumeration disagree by one; when reproducing, trust the enumeration of smearing powers, not the total '14'.

items.claim: Target 7 (D=4 leading-log): g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2) * 25 * log(0.3 M b_max), eq (70), footnote 15; WARNING that arXiv v1 of this equation is numerically wrong (incorrect p_min <-> b_max relation), use v2/JHEP
items.detail: v2 source has exactly g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2) x 25 log(0.3 M b_max) (D=4), i.e. coefficients 25 and 0.3, with the corrected relation p_min^2 = c/(M b_max^3), c ~ 1. Footnote confirmed to be number 15 in the compiled paper (verified via pdftotext of the compiled v2 PDF) with text 'An earlier arXiv version of this paper used an incorrect relation between p_min with b_max, which resulted in a numerically incorrect bound.' ADVERSARIAL CHECK OF THE WARNING ITSELF: I downloaded the actual arXiv v1 e-print (Feb 17 2021) and its sec_localized_b.tex line 358 reads g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2) x 17 log(1.7 M b_max), derived from the incorrect relation p_min ~ 1/b_max (v1 line 351). So the v1 values were 17 and 1.7, materially different from v2's 25 and 0.3 — the warning is real and load-bearing. Agreed this is scheme-dependent and lowest priority as an exact reproduction target.

items.claim: Target 8 (string reference points): type II dilaton amplitude eq (58) has g_2=g_3=0, g_4 = 4piG zeta(3)/m^6, i.e. g_4M^6/(8piG) = x zeta(3)/2 with x=(M/m)^6 in (0,1]; spin-0-subtracted in D=6: g_2 m^2/(8piG) ~ -0.75, g_3 m^4/(8piG) ~ -2.07; heterotic dilatons g_2 m^2/(8piG) = 3/16, g_3 m^4/(8piG) = 3/4; all strictly inside Table 3 region, far from saturation
items.detail: (a) Type II: fully recomputed symbolically. M_string - graviton = 2 zeta(3) [(st)^2+(tu)^2+(su)^2] + O(deg 10) and with s+t+u=0 one has (st)^2+(tu)^2+(su)^2 = (s^2+t^2+u^2)^2/4, giving exactly g_2=g_3=0, g_4 = (zeta(3)/2)(8piG)/m^6 = 4piG zeta(3)/m^6; next corrections are zeta(5)-level (>=10-derivative), so g_2=g_3=0 is exact. x=(M/m)^6 parametrization consistent with paper's 'any 0 < x <= 1' since m >= M. (b) D=6 subtracted values: INDEPENDENTLY RECOMPUTED from scratch — extracted residues of M_string at s=n (n=1..10), projected onto the D=6 Gegenbauer J=0 component (c_{n,0} = (3/4) int R_n(x)(1-x^2)dx), and summed Delta g_2 = -sum c_{n,0}/n^3, Delta g_3 = -3 sum c_{n,0}/n^4: converged to g_2 m^2/(8piG) = -0.748, g_3 m^4/(8piG) = -2.066, matching the quoted ~-0.75 and ~-2.07 (paper's ~ precision). (c) Heterotic 3/16 and 3/4: verbatim in source (eq 3.28 = global 64) — extraction confirmed, but NOT independently recomputed (would require the KLT heterotic four-dilaton amplitude; no citing source found quoting these numbers). (d) 'Strictly inside, far from saturation' verified numerically against Table 3 (D=6, worst margins with M=m): type II subtracted point satisfies g_2+0.0940931 g_3+4.55838 = 3.61 > 0 and slope-3 edge margin 28.0; heterotic point margins ~3.8 and ~27.9 — both far inside.

items.claim: Parameters block: units M=1, 8piG=1 (eqs 80-81); u in [-M^2,0], p in [0,1]; Appendix B u_min/M^2 in {-1,-5/4,-5/3,-5/2} with 54-dim space, meromorphic amplitudes only; Table 1 smearing bases (D=5: p^3-p^2,...; even D>=6: p^{3/2},p^{5/2},...; odd D>=7: p^2,p^3,...) chosen for positive large-b tail and convergence requiring n>1; h_k in nonneg integer powers; closed-form 2F1 p-integrals (eq 83, J=2); Fig 4 grid m^2=1/(1-x), dx=1/400; Bessel action eqs (85)-(86), b-grid eps_b=1/250, db=1/32, B=40, 2x2 PSD condition eqs (88)-(90) with m_max=2; adaptive refinement (~2N+1 points, N=10, dips always occur, iterate to ~1e-6; Fig 8 final negative region width 2.2e-6, depth -1.83e-10); 40 rays theta in {0,...,39pi/20}; Fig 6 settings (93->92 dim up to X_14, J_max=150, dx=1/800, db=1/100, B=80, m_max=6, extra x-constraints {1-4/J,...,1-1/(50J)} for J=36..150); SUGRA 14 functionals
items.detail: Every item verified verbatim against app_flat_space_numerics.tex, app_extended_u.tex, and Table 1/Table 2 in the v2 source: A.1-A.2 set M=1, 8piG=1; basis-function table exact; n>1 convergence requirement and the large-b positivity rationale (1F2 asymptotics, need n <= (D-3)/2 or cancellation of leading oscillatory term — motivating D=5 differences p^k - p^2) exact; example 2F1 integral is indeed for J=2 (A.4); x-grid {0, dx, ...} with dx=1/400 (Fig 4) and 1/800 (Fig 6); b-grid eps_b=1/250, db=1/32 (Fig 4) / 1/100 (Fig 6), B=40/80, m_max=2/6; 2x2 PSD matrix ((A+B, C),(C, A-B)) >= 0 for b > B on truncated asymptotic expansions; refinement inserts {x*-Ns,...,x*+Ns} (2N+1 points) with s=|x2-x1|/N, N=10, iterated until negative regions ~1e-6, and Fig 8 caption gives final negative region b in (10.7298112, 10.7298134) — width 2.2e-6 — with minimum -1.83e-10; 40 rays theta in {0, pi/20, ..., 39pi/20} from a point near the tip; extra x-constraints for J=36..150 and the 93->92 g_3-blind projection in Table 2 caption; Appendix B: u_min/M^2 = -1, -5/4, -5/3, -5/2 with the 54-dim space, explicitly restricted to meromorphic amplitudes (extended-u validity argument). Also confirmed: SDPB precision=768 (Fig 4) and 840 with 1e-80 thresholds (Fig 6), and functional tables built in Mathematica at 300 digits — worth knowing for reproduction. Only caveat: the '17-dim'/'14 functionals' counting glosses flagged under Targets 3 and 6.
